questions.question: In the number $456$, what is the place value of the digit $5$?
Round the number $234.67$ to the nearest $10$.
A group of $247$ hikers are going on a trek. Express this number in expanded form.
A wildlife reserve has $1,562$ animals. What is the place value of the digit $6$?
A car consumes $14.25$ gallons of fuel per $100$ miles. Round this to the nearest $0.1$ gallon.
A hiking trail is $5,678$ feet long. Express this number in standard form.

options.A: Five thousand six hundred seventy eight
options.C: Five thousand seven hundred sixty eight

questions.question: A park ranger travels $123.45$ miles in a day. Round this to the nearest mile.
A bird sanctuary has $819$ birds. What is the place value of the digit $9$?
A hiker travels a distance of $456.78$ miles. Express this number in expanded form and round it to the nearest $10$. Show your work and provide your final answer.

A group of friends want to go on a $2,345.67$-mile road trip. If their car consumes $25.34$ miles per gallon, how many gallons of fuel will they need for the trip, rounded to the nearest $0.1$ gallon? Show your work and provide your final answer.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of place value before attempting this test.
Tip 2: Encourage students to use the standard form of numbers when expressing their answers.
Tip 3: Remind students to show their work and provide explanations for their answers in the free-response questions.
